title: overlay.go client.TickStatus: defer unlock + panic recovery
description:
File: internal/session/client/overlay.go, lines 141-143

client.TickStatus() runs as a bare goroutine (go c.TickStatus(stopStatus), line 228). It ticks every 1 second to re-render the status bar. The VT mutex is locked/unlocked manually each tick.

Current risk: If RenderStatusBar() panics, the goroutine dies, the mutex stays locked (deadlocking the session), and the status bar stops updating. No recover, not relaunched.

Fix:
1. Convert manual vt.Mu.Unlock() to defer-based unlock (wrap in anonymous func).
2. Add a recover() inside the for-loop tick case so a single bad render doesn't kill the ticker — it should log the panic and continue ticking.
